pattern: If some value plays the role of the special numbers' sum, the outlier is forced to equal the total minus twice that value, so checking every candidate sum against a frequency table finds every possible outlier.
hints: Call the total of all n numbers T. If some element x is the one representing the sum of the special numbers, then the outlier must equal T - 2*x (because T = specials-sum + x + outlier = x + x + outlier).
Build a frequency count of the array's values so you can quickly check whether a given candidate outlier value actually exists elsewhere in the array.

For every distinct value x, test candidate = T - 2*x; if candidate appears in the array at an index that can differ from x's (watch the case candidate == x, which needs x to appear at least twice), track the largest valid candidate.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, straightforward sum and outlier
statement example, more than two special numbers possible
statement example, sum value and outlier share the same numeric value
candidate equal to the sum-element's own value needs a second occurrence to be valid
multiple candidate outliers exist, must pick the largest
values at the negative and positive extremes

stress.description: random specials plus a constructed sum element and outlier, shuffled
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    m = max(1, min(198, size))
    specials = [rng.randint(-1000, 1000) for _ in range(m)]
    s = sum(specials)
    s = max(-1000, min(1000, s))
    outlier = rng.randint(-1000, 1000)
    nums = specials + [s, outlier]
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    return [nums]
